Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–66, div. B, title XXIX, § 2945127 Stat. 1038Dec. 26, 2013“(a).—DesignationThere is hereby designated the ‘Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area’, consisting of—“(1)43,431 acres (as depicted on the map referred to in subsection (b) of section 2941 []) of the existing Bureau of Land Management-designated Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Area that is not withdrawn and reserved for defense-related uses by such section; and127 Stat. 1034“(2)The Shared Use Area.“(b).—Authorized ActivitiesTo the extent consistent with applicable Federal law (including regulations) and this subtitle [subtitle C (§§ 2941–2946) of title XXIX of div. B of , see Tables for classification] any authorized recreation activities and use designation in effect on the date of enactment of this Act [] and applicable to the Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area may continue, including casual off-highway vehicular use and recreation.Pub. L. 113–66Dec. 26, 2013“(c).—AdministrationThe Secretary of the Interior shall administer the Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area (other than the Shared Use Area, which is being managed in accordance with the other provisions of this subtitle) in accordance with—“(1)the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.); and43 U.S.C. 1701“(2)any other applicable law.“(d).—TransitIn coordination with the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of the Navy may authorize transit through the Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area for defense-related purposes supporting military training (including military range management and management of exercise activities) conducted on the land withdrawn and reserved by section 2941.”[For definition of “Shared Use Area” as used in , set out above, see , , , which is not classified to the Code.]section 2945 of Pub. children.heading: Dumont Dunes Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.label: A Dumont Dunes Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.text: Certain Bureau of Land Management land in the Conservation Area, comprising approximately 7,620 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Dumont Dunes OHV Recreation Area” and dated , which shall be known as the “Dumont Dunes Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area”.November 7, 2018

children.heading: El Mirage Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.label: B El Mirage Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.text: Certain Bureau of Land Management land in the Conservation Area, comprising approximately 16,370 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed El Mirage OHV Recreation Area” and dated , which shall be known as the “El Mirage Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area”.December 10, 2018

children.heading: Rasor Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.label: C Rasor Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.text: Certain Bureau of Land Management land in the Conservation Area, comprising approximately 23,900 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Rasor OHV Recreation Area” and dated , which shall be known as the “Rasor Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area”.November 7, 2018

children.heading: Spangler Hills Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.label: D Spangler Hills Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.text: Certain Bureau of Land Management land in the Conservation Area, comprising approximately 92,340 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Spangler Hills OHV Recreation Area” and dated , which shall be known as the “Spangler Hills Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area”.December 10, 2018

children.heading: Stoddard Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.label: E Stoddard Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.text: Certain Bureau of Land Management land in the Conservation Area, comprising approximately 40,110 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Stoddard Valley OHV Recreation Area” and dated , which shall be known as the “Stoddard Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area”.November 7, 2018

children.heading: Expansion of Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.label: 2 Expansion of Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area
children.text: The Johnson Valley Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area designated by section 2945 of the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2014 (division B of ; ) is expanded to include approximately 20,240 acres, depicted as “Proposed OHV Recreation Area Additions” and “Proposed OHV Recreation Area Study Areas” on the map entitled “Proposed Johnson Valley OHV Recreation Area” and dated .Public Law 113–66127 Stat. 1038November 7, 2018

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: b Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the off-highway vehicle recreation areas designated or expanded under subsection (a) is to preserve and enhance the recreational opportunities within the Conservation Area (including opportunities for off-highway vehicle recreation), while conserving the wildlife and other natural resource values of the Conservation Area.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate.

children.heading: Legal effect
children.label: 2 Legal effect
children.text: The map and legal descriptions of the off-highway vehicle recreation areas filed under paragraph (1) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this part, except that the Secretary may correct errors in the map and legal descriptions.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 3 Public availability
children.text: Each map and legal description filed under paragraph (1) shall be filed and made available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Bureau of Land Management.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to authorize, maintain, and enhance the recreational uses of the off-highway vehicle recreation areas designated or expanded by subsection (a), as long as the recreational use is consistent with this section and any other applicable law.

children.heading: Off-highway vehicle and off-highway recreation
children.label: B Off-highway vehicle and off-highway recreation
children.text: To the extent consistent with applicable Federal law (including regulations) and this section, any authorized recreation activities and use designations in effect on , and applicable to the off-highway vehicle recreation areas designated or expanded by subsection (a) shall continue, including casual off-highway vehicular use, racing, competitive events, rock crawling, training, and other forms of off-highway recreation.March 12, 2019

children.label: A
children.text: applicable Bureau of Land Management guidelines; and

children.label: B
children.text: State law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), commercial development (including development of energy facilities, but excluding energy transport facilities, rights-of-way, and related telecommunication facilities) shall be prohibited in the off-highway vehicle recreation areas designated or expanded by subsection (a) if the Secretary determines that the development is incompatible with the purpose described in subsection (b).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The Secretary may issue a temporary permit to a commercial vendor to provide accessories and other support for off-highway vehicle use in an off-highway vehicle recreation area designated or expanded by subsection (a) for a limited period and consistent with the purposes of the off-highway vehicle recreation area and applicable laws.

children.heading: Interim plans
children.label: C Interim plans
children.text: Pending completion of a new management plan under subparagraph (A), the existing resource management plans shall govern the use of the applicable off-highway vehicle recreation area.

right-of-way, leasing, or disposition under all laws relating to mineral leasing, geothermal leasing, or mineral materials.

children.heading: Pacific Gas and Electric Company utility facilities and rights-of-way
children.label: h Pacific Gas and Electric Company utility facilities and rights-of-way
A
children.text: affects any validly issued right-of-way for the customary operation, maintenance, upgrade, repair, relocation within an existing right-of-way, replacement, or other authorized activity (including the use of any mechanized vehicle, helicopter, and other aerial device) in a right-of-way acquired by or issued, granted, or permitted to Pacific Gas and Electric Company (including any successor in interest or assign) that is located on land included in the Spangler Hills Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area; or

children.heading: Plans for access
children.label: 2 Plans for access
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , or the issuance of a new utility facility right-of-way within the Spangler Hills Off-Highway Vehicle Recreation Area, whichever is later, the Secretary, in consultation with the Pacific Gas and Electric Company, shall publish plans for regular and emergency access by the Pacific Gas and Electric Company to the rights-of-way of the Pacific Gas and Electric Company.March 12, 2019
